entities.name: Simulation-First Development
entities.context: Core principle: simulate before building to reveal problems early, 20 minutes thinking saves 4 hours coding

entities.name: Prose Simulation
entities.context: Technique using markdown specs, architecture diagrams, and scenario walkthroughs to explore before coding

entities.name: Prototype Simulation
entities.context: Testing approaches with throwaway code (e.g., JSONL vs SQLite) to answer technical questions

entities.name: Throwaway Prototype
entities.context: Code explicitly meant to be deleted after learning; must use /tmp, never keep

entities.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
entities.context: Anti-pattern where investment in wrong approach prevents switching; avoided by simulating first

entities.name: Analysis Paralysis
entities.context: Anti-pattern of over-simulating; diminishing returns after 30 minutes

entities.name: JSONL
entities.context: File format option for data storage, evaluated via prototype simulation

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database option for data storage, evaluated via prototype simulation

entities.name: D2
entities.context: Diagramming tool for architecture diagrams in prose simulation

entities.name: Mermaid
entities.context: Diagramming tool for architecture diagrams in prose simulation

entities.name: Jupyter Notebooks
entities.context: Tool for code exploration and simulation

entities.name: Markdown
entities.context: Format for prose specifications and scenario walkthroughs

relationships.context: Prose simulation is a technique within simulation-first approach
Prototype simulation is a technique within simulation-first approach
JSONL evaluated as format option in prototype
SQLite evaluated as storage option in prototype
Simulation-first approach prevents sunk cost fallacy by revealing problems early
Over-simulating is an anti-pattern; 30 min max recommended
